articles.title: Telangana Thunderstorm Alert as Rain Brings Relief from Heatwave - INDToday
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad, April 20 (Maxim News) : After days of intense heat across Telangana, several districts received rain that brought temporary relief to residents. However, weather experts have now issued a fresh alert for thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds across many parts of the state.
Here To Join Us On WhatsApp
For the past few days, temperatures in Telangana stayed above 40 degrees Celsius. In some areas, the mercury touched 44 to 45 degrees, causing severe discomfort. As a result, people faced harsh summer conditions during daytime hours.
Now, according to the latest forecast, heavy thunderstorms may hit eastern, central, and western Telangana districts. Officials warned that strong weather activity is likely over the next few hours.
Telangana Thunderstorm Alert for Key Districts
Districts likely to be affected include Nalgonda, Yadadri Bhuvanagiri, Jangaon, Warangal, Rangareddy, Nagarkurnool, Medak, and Sangareddy. These areas may witness thunder, lightning, and possible strikes.
Here To Join Us On WhatsApp
In addition, some places may receive hailstorm activity. Strong winds are also expected, which could damage weak structures, tree branches, and hoardings.
Meanwhile, Hyderabad is also likely to witness unstable weather conditions similar to the previous day. Around 50 percent of the city may experience storm impact. Therefore, residents have been asked to remain alert, especially between evening and midnight.
Here To Join Us On WhatsApp
Authorities advised people not to stand under trees during rain. They also warned residents to stay away from electric poles and wires during thunderstorms.
Motorists should drive carefully as sudden rain and winds may reduce visibility. Waterlogging may also occur in low lying areas.
Weather officials further advised citizens to follow local alerts and avoid unnecessary travel during storm hours. Though the rain may lower temperatures, safety remains the top priority. (Maxim News)

articles.title: Telangana mandals report over 1,500 heat wave cases in 10 years - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: Over the past decade, scorching temperatures have gripped vast parts of the state, with almost 1,560 heatwave cases recorded in mandals between 2015 and 2025. Yet, Hyderabad has largely escaped the worst of this, with just nine mandals experiencing heatwaves during this time.
According to the recent analysis by the planning department, the most recent instance of a heat wave in the city occurred in 2023, affecting just one mandal, while several mandals saw little to no heat wave activity between 2015 and 2025.
Experts attribute this trend partly to the design of older neighbourhoods in the city. Areas with thick walls, high ceilings, and abundant greenery—such as parts of Jubilee Hills—naturally regulate indoor temperatures and offer better cooling. In contrast, newer urban zones like Hitec City tend to trap heat due to glass-heavy, high-rise constructions, creating what is often described as "vertical heat."
Unlike coastal cities such as Vijayawada and Kolkata, which face high humidity that creates a "pressure cooker" effect, Hyderabad typically experiences dry heat. This dryness allows sweat to evaporate more efficiently, helping the body cool itself, although experts note that the humidity levels have been gradually changing.
Last year, the city recorded temperatures of around 42°C.
Nestled on the Deccan Plateau, Hyderabad benefits from a higher elevation compared to the surrounding plains, where temperatures often climb higher. While daytime conditions can be sweltering, the city usually enjoys a noticeable drop in temperature at night, offering some relief.
Based on average temperature data from 2015 to 2025, the state recorded a mean maximum temperature of 34.0°C.
During the peak summer months, temperatures increased to a scorching 38.5°C observed over the period 2015–2025, creating intense and often challenging heat conditions.
As the season transitioned into the northeast monsoon, temperatures eased, with mean values dropping to a more comfortable 32.0°C.
Call for traditional cooling methods
Officials have announced a Heat Action Plan (HAP) that focuses on localised, data-driven solutions. Residents are being encouraged to adopt traditional hydration practices such as drinking buttermilk (majiga) and lemon water (nimbu paani), and to use air coolers instead of relying entirely on air conditioning.
Meanwhile, neighbouring districts have been more severely affected. Nalgonda district has recorded the highest number of heat-affected mandals (150), followed by Mancherial district with 143.
In light of the impending heat wave action plan, it is significant to note that all districts have received above-normal rainfall over the past six months.
